<!-- Generated by patchloom v0.1.0 — https://github.com/patchloom/patchloom -->
# Patchloom

Patchloom is a CLI for structured file operations that your native agent tools cannot do safely: parser-backed JSON/YAML/TOML editing, heading-aware markdown editing, atomic multi-file transactions, and unified diff application.

For simple operations (read, search, string replace, create, delete), use your native agent tools. They are faster.

## Commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `doc` | Parser-backed JSON/YAML/TOML: get, set, delete, merge, append, prepend, update, move, ensure, flatten, diff |
| `md` | Heading-aware markdown edits: replace-section, insert, upsert-bullet, table-append, dedupe |
| `tx` | Execute a multi-operation plan atomically with format and validate steps |
| `patch` | Apply unified diffs with stale-context detection |
| `hygiene` | Final newline, line ending, and trailing whitespace normalization |

## When to use patchloom

| Task | Command | Why not native tools |
|---|---|---|
| Edit JSON, YAML, or TOML by key | `patchloom doc set config.json key value --apply` | Parser-backed, type-safe, preserves formatting. `search_replace` can break syntax. |
| Merge fields into a config | `patchloom doc merge config.yaml --value '{"db":{"host":"localhost"}}' --apply` | Deep merge without clobbering existing keys. |
| Append to a markdown table | `patchloom md table-append --file README.md --heading "## Commands" --row "\| cmd \| desc \|" --apply` | Heading-aware, finds the right table. `search_replace` on tables is fragile. |
| Replace a markdown section | `patchloom md replace-section --file CHANGELOG.md --heading "## Unreleased" --content "new text" --apply` | Replaces only the section body, preserves everything else. |
| Add a bullet idempotently | `patchloom md upsert-bullet --file AGENTS.md --heading "## Rules" --bullet "- New rule" --apply` | Skips if already present. |
| Edit 3+ files at once | `patchloom tx --plan plan.json --apply` | **One tool call instead of N.** Batches doc/replace/create/md ops into a single plan. Also provides rollback and format/validate lifecycle. Always prefer `tx` over repeated individual calls when editing multiple files. |
| Apply a unified diff | `patchloom patch apply --stdin --apply` | Stale context detection (exit 5 on mismatch). |
| Check for whitespace drift | `patchloom hygiene check .` | Exit 2 when issues found. CI-friendly. |

## Write modes

All write commands default to preview. Nothing is written unless you pass `--apply`.

| Flag | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `--diff` (default) | Print unified diff of what would change |
| `--check` | Exit 0 if clean, exit 2 if changes pending |
| `--apply` | Write changes to disk |

## Exit codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 0 | Success |
| 1 | General error |
| 2 | Changes detected (`--check` mode) |
| 3 | No matches found |
| 5 | Stale patch context |
| 6 | Validation failed |
| 7 | Rollback (strict mode) |

## Transaction plans

The `tx` command runs multiple operations atomically with optional format and validate steps.

```json
{
  "strict": true,
  "operations": [
    { "op": "doc.set", "path": "config.json", "key": "version", "value": "2.0" },
    { "op": "md.upsert_bullet", "path": "AGENTS.md", "heading": "Rules", "bullet": "- New rule" },
    { "op": "replace", "path": "src/main.rs", "from": "old", "to": "new" },
    { "op": "file.create", "path": "new.txt", "content": "hello" }
  ],
  "format": [
    { "cmd": "cargo fmt --all", "timeout": 30 }
  ],
  "validate": [
    { "cmd": "make check", "required": true, "timeout": 120 }
  ]
}
```

With `"strict": true`, a format or validation failure reverts all writes (exit 7). Plans accept JSON, YAML, and TOML formats.

## Security

Plans execute shell commands in `format` and `validate` steps with the user's full privileges. Treat plan files with the same care as shell scripts.
